description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the microsoft/dotnet Docker image with two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add a build job that runs git submodule init then dotnet build. In the deploy stage add a deploy job that first ensures ssh-agent is available (install openssh-client and git if missing), runs git submodule init, publishes the project for linux-x64, win-x86, win-x64, and osx-x64 with dotnet publish -c Release --self-contained --runtime <runtime>, starts ssh-agent, adds the $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, prepares the ~/.ssh directory, adds the $DEPLOYMENT_SERVER_IP to known_hosts, creates remote directories via ssh webmaster@$DEPLOYMENT_SERVER_IP, and copies each built binary to the corresponding remote path using scp. The deploy job runs on any branch and deploys the binaries to a remote server over SSH.
